ops.title: 2026-07-14 Arc cookie wipe — kill-storm tore live DBs, Chromium razed; union self-healed once key warmed; cookiesync exonerated; F1-F6 shipped v0.13.0, F7 v0.13.1; SEQUEL: login-clobber root-caused (blind upsert + converge TOCTOU) and fixed v0.14.1 (mono

ops.body:
2026-07-14 ~00:30 PDT: an EXTERNAL actor (closed as unidentifiable — complete window sweep cleared all 19 in-window Claude sessions of any browser/`open` activity; zero Arc crash reports so the relaunches were clean quits not crash-loops; Arc binary unchanged since Jul 10 so not the updater; unified log rotated; NOT a Claude session despite appearances) kill/relaunched Arc until Chromium's integrity check razed ALL Arc profiles' cookie DBs in place (rows deleted, files kept, same-second mtimes). Keychain "Arc Safe Storage" untouched. cookiesync EXONERATED of corruption: reads are copy-then-read (never opens live DB), writes upsert-only, key was cold all night. The daemon-log `wal checkpoint deadline` / `malformed (11)` / `near "FROM"` lines are SPURIOUS torn-copy noise, not live damage (near-FROM = empty-columns Sprintf in stores.go tableColumns/upsertSQL — real bug).

REJECTED THEORY (don't resurrect): a forensics sweep concluded "converge write-back wiped Arc" — impossible twice over: Profile 1/2 are UNTRACKED (no converge path touches them) and Write is INSERT…ON CONFLICT upsert with no DELETE (cannot zero a store). Converge write-backs at 06:42 were the HEAL, not damage. Deploy timeline: session 3d72af98 brew-upgraded this Mac to v0.12.0 at 06:26–06:28 PDT; its install + routed-consent taps (06:29/06:30) warmed keys and the fresh daemon's converge restored Arc (~06:30–06:42). me@laptop residue = pre-Jul-8 test-fixture tombstone (added_at=0, all refs test-only), no fresh leak — current test helpers isolate correctly.

The cold-key gate accidentally firewalled the incident (no propagation, no heal); by ~06:30 a warm key converge SELF-HEALED Arc from the union (9,420 rows, 7,921 pre-wipe-era; peer identical) — additive union, newest-wins, NO tombstones (merge.go): absence never deletes, an emptied store gets restored. Post-heal backups: `~/cookie-rescue-20260714/` on both hosts. Permanent losses: Arc Profile 1/2 cookies (untracked by user decision — spaces keep separate identities) + the ~15:00–22:10 Jul 13 capture gap.

**Why:** next wipe-looking incident: check creation_utc era split + key-warm state before assuming propagation; the union restores on next warm converge — don't panic-restore by hand.

**How to apply:** hardening SHIPPED 2026-07-14 as cookiesync v0.13.0 + synckit v0.14.0, deployed both Macs (F1 VACUUM INTO snapshot reads + ErrStoreBusy hot-journal classification; F2 empty-columns SQL guards; F3 WatchItem.Busy via SingletonLock+kill-0 + IMMEDIATE writes with logged skip; F4 durable rowcount-baseline quarantine at Converge; F5 hostregistry.Config.DirEnv → COOKIESYNC_CONFIG_DIR + testutil.IsolateHostConfig; F6 cregistry.Compact 30d horizon at Driver.SaveRegistry). F7 SHIPPED 2026-07-14 PM (cookiesync v0.13.1 = 114fefe8, implemented in an isolated worktree off main, NOT the bridge session): approverUnavailableError classifies approver probe failures unavailable → structured {"status":"unavailable"} failover; probeRoutesAround (DeadlineExceeded or any *hostregistry.SSHError = peer-not-live) on the whoami leg in BOTH routedRelease's loop and routesConsent's hard-route preflight (the second call site was a codex security-review catch); union total-shutout error now carries the warnings + "consent may be pending on <hosts>". Error-5 papercut FIXED in synckit 194701e (retry bootout→bootstrap on EIO; binary released as v0.15.1 — the v0.15.0 tag sat off-main on bcdd8ef, release gate refused it, module tag stays put, never move a published Go module tag) — verified live: upgrade-in-place synckitd install exit 0 on both Macs incl. yasyf-home. ccx crash-loop FIXED in cc-context v0.17.3 (SessionStart brew arms under a best-effort mkdir lock). Both Macs deployed 2026-07-14 PM: cookiesync 0.13.1 + synckitd 0.15.1 + ccx 0.17.3, doctor 8/8. Post-deploy key caches cold — first auth tap re-warms. Original queue framing: F1–F6 (atomic clonefile snapshots + ro/immutable reads; empty-columns SQL guard; WatchItem.Busy liveness gate + BEGIN IMMEDIATE; durable mass-drop quarantine; COOKIESYNC_CONFIG_DIR isolation; endpoint identity dedup — registry holds short-name AND FQDN duplicate keys, the gap that keeps phantoms alive). Plus regressions for the routed-consent failures seen live: consent leg dying as `run ioreg: signal: killed` instead of bounded failover; cold get_cookies not attempting the configured route. Fixes build ON DEPLOYED v0.12.0 (this Mac); yasyf-home still on old version — deploy there rides the ship step. Re-anchor converge/daemon cites on the v0.12.0 tree first. See [[cookiesync-architecture]], [[cookiesync-union-hang-and-deadline-fixes]].

**SEQUEL (2026-07-15 evening): the reproducible login clobber.** A fresh claude.ai login in Arc died on every quit/relaunch — root cause was NOT the busy gate (amplifier only) but the blind `INSERT…ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` upsert + converge's extract→SSH(10s–4m46s)→apply TOCTOU + a self-perpetuating freeze (stale disk row is never dirty in the browser, sibling churn keeps rowSetEqual false, every pass re-plants it — write-once login cookies freeze, churny ones self-heal). FIXED in cookiesync v0.14.1 (a9d21f37d226, deployed both Macs 2026-07-15 PM): monotone upserts (`WHERE excluded.last_update_utc > cookies.last_update_utc`, schema-aware, strict >, applied = summed RowsAffected) + session/expired rows filtered from converge gather and inbound apply (export paths unfiltered). Review accepted two edges: equal-ts/different-content never converges (needs same-microsecond writes), and quarantine restore is best-effort under monotone writes (regenerated-fresher keys keep LWW). Full record: cc-notes note 917ade59. Live drills (Touch ID + login repro) were PENDING user at session end. Cross-host gotcha discovered: this Mac's local TZ renders UTC+12, yasyf-home PDT — compare `ps lstart`/logs in UTC only, else you fabricate 19-hour anomalies.
